result.stdout:
trrename
Copyright (c) 2023 Ken Domino
# trrename

## Summary

Rename symbols in a grammar

## Description

Rename symbols in a grammar.

## Usage

    trrename -r <string>

## Details

`trrename` renames rule symbols in a grammar.

The `-r` option is required. It
is a list of semi-colon delimited pairs of symbol names, which are separated
by a comma, e.g., `id,identifier;name,name_`. If you are using Bash,
make sure to enclose the argument as it contains semi-colons.

## Examples

    trparse Foobar.g4 | trrename -r "a,b;c,d" | trprint > new-grammar.g4

## Current version

0.23.43 Fixes to trgen templates.

## License

  -e, --expr               (Default: //(parserRuleSpec |
                           lexerRuleSpec)//(RULE_REF | TOKEN_REF))
  -f, --file               Read parse tree data from file instead of stdin.
  --fmt                    Output formatted parsing results set.
  -R, --rename-map-file    
  -v, --verbose            
  --version                
  --help                   Display this help screen.
  --version                Display version information.
  value pos. 0
